chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P.No. D- 2028 of 2010 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case(Priority) 1.For orders on CMA 986/15 2.For hearing of CMA 6179/15 3.For hearing of CMA 6180/15. 4.For hearing of main case. 31-05-2022 Mr. Shabbir Ali Bozdar, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Manoj Kumar Tejwani, Advocate for Respondent No.5/PPHI. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Naich, Assistant A.G a/w Usman Abdullah, Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki. ***** Pursuant to Order dated 11.05.2022, the Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki today has filed report along with various documents and in the report it has been stated that the rehabilitation of Rural Health Centre, Jarwar is being taken up with estimated cost of Rs. 37.0762 million and PC-1/rough cost estimate has been annexed with this report. It is further submitted that scheme will be taken up in the next phase after putting up before the Petroleum Social Development Committee and Social Welfare Committee for approval, out of Production Bonus and Social Welfare Obligatory Fund provided by the E&P Companies. It is further requested that due to Elections, wherein the District Administration is engaged, the matter has been delayed, and therefore, sufficient time be granted to process the approval of PC-1/estimate. This petition is pending since 2010, and the main grievance of the petitioner is in respect of denial of basic health facilities at the Rural Health Centre, Jarwar, District Ghotki, and it is an unfortunate state of affairs insofar as the Government of Sindh is concerned. Despite the fact, that an initiative has been taken by establishing an NGO i.e.

This petition is pending since 2010, and the main grievance of the petitioner is in respect of denial of basic health facilities at the Rural Health Centre, Jarwar, District Ghotki, and it is an unfortunate state of affairs insofar as the Government of Sindh is concerned. Despite the fact, that an initiative has been taken by establishing an NGO i.e. Public Private Health Initiative (“PPHI”) who is also a respondent herein, no resources have been allocated or provided for maintaining any such health facility in a satisfactory manner. Learned Counsel for PPHI on one of the dates of hearing had also submitted that despite their best effort no formal support in any manner is being provided to them by the Government. In fact, even Page 2 of 2 after filing of this petition, the Government has remained aloof and finally even if some efforts have been made by the Deputy Commissioner, and that too after passing of strict orders by this Court, it is from the funds of private section companies, and not from the official funds allocated by the Government in its annual budget. The Chief Secretary and Secretary Health, are directed to look into this issue and shall ensure appropriate remedial measures at their end, as far as possible. In view of such position and on the basis of report today filed by the Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki, this Petition is disposed of with directions to the Respondents as well as Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki, to abide by the above statement and make compliance of the same in letter and spirit. As to the approval of the P.C-1, let such exercise be completed by the Respondents within a period of 60-days from today and a periodical report shall be filed by the Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki, with the Additional Registrar of this Court as to satisfactory compliance of the above statement as well as orders of this Court; whereas, the Petitioner may file appropriate application if orders are not complied with. This Petition stands disposed of in the above terms along with pending applications.

As to the approval of the P.C-1, let such exercise be completed by the Respondents within a period of 60-days from today and a periodical report shall be filed by the Deputy Commissioner, Ghotki, with the Additional Registrar of this Court as to satisfactory compliance of the above statement as well as orders of this Court; whereas, the Petitioner may file appropriate application if orders are not complied with. This Petition stands disposed of in the above terms along with pending applications. Let copy of this Order be sent to all concerned including AAG’s office. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
